title: AstraZeneca — Oncology — briefing Q3-2025
html:
AstraZeneca Oncology — Q3 2025 Briefing

The Single Most Important Thing This Quarter

Datroway's US approval in EGFR-mutated NSCLC (June 2025) gives AZ its first ADC foothold in lung cancer, setting the stage for the 1L NSCLC franchise play. While the mBC launch (Jan 2025) was the commercial milestone, the NSCLC accelerated approval validates TROP2 as a target in lung and builds the clinical narrative for the TROPION-Lung 1L trials.

Key Developments

Imfinzi Franchise

POSEIDON Messaging Gains Traction (Q3 2025 PET Data):

  • POSEIDON reps effectively prioritized STK11m (IM13) and KRASm (IM14) efficacy messages — both recalled above industry average
  • Average recall was 33% with 3.5 messages recalled per interaction — solid performance
  • POSEIDON likelihood to increase prescribing was building momentum through Q3
  • Imfinzi's sales rep ratings were favorable vs competitors, with overall call quality directionally leading industry average
  • New messages on KEAP1m (IM23), biomarker context (IM18-21) introduced — perceived as most motivating but early in recall build

AEGEAN Holding Steady:

  • AEGEAN messages recalled at higher rates (36% average, 3.5 per interaction) but effectiveness not yet differentiated from competitors
  • All AEGEAN messages except "4x more patients" (IMF14) perceived as less motivating than industry average
  • Perioperative space remains competitive with Keytruda (KEYNOTE-671) and Opdivo (CheckMate 77T)

PACIFIC Stable:

  • Five-year data narrative continues to anchor the franchise
  • Most messages recalled at similar rates — opportunity for greater prioritization of highest-impact messages emerging
  • PACIFIC-R real-world data (42% of PD-L1<1% patients alive at 5 years) provides important real-world confirmation

SCLC — Pre-IMforte Baseline:

  • This quarter represents the last clean SCLC competitive landscape before IMforte approval
  • Imfinzi led Tecentriq in SOV across target and all MDs
  • ADRIATIC messages were beginning to build recall and resonance following Dec 2024 FDA approval

Datroway / Dato-DXd

NSCLC Accelerated Approval (June 2025):

  • FDA granted accelerated approval for previously treated EGFR-mutated mNSCLC based on pooled TROPION-Lung05/01 data
  • ORR 45% (95% CI: 35-54), median DoR 6.5 months
  • First TROP2-directed therapy approved for EGFR+ NSCLC
  • Commercial significance: builds oncologist familiarity with Datroway in the NSCLC setting ahead of 1L trials

mBC Launch Momentum:

  • Datroway continued to build SOV in HER2-/HER2-low market through Q3
  • Rep reach and frequency stabilizing at competitive levels vs Enhertu and Trodelvy
  • TROP2-Breast01 data increasingly cited at conferences

1L NSCLC Pipeline:

  • Four Phase III trials (Dato-DXd + pembro/durva +/- chemo, Dato-DXd + rilvegostomig) enrolling across PD-L1 subtypes
  • Interim US qualitative insights (ZoomRx topline) confirmed physician enthusiasm, especially in PD-L1 Low/Negative
  • Key concern surface: grade 3+ AE rates and discontinuation rates perceived as markedly higher than current SoC

Lynparza

  • Continued steady revenue contribution (~$3.3B run rate)
  • No major new developments; market position stable

Company Context

  • AZ on track for strong FY2025 with oncology revenue growth ~17% (ex-milestones)
  • Imfinzi tracking toward $6B+ for the full year
  • $80B revenue target affirmed at JPM26 as "very much within reach"

Client Financial Signals — AZ Q3 2025 Earnings Call

AZ total revenue ~$13.5B (+15%). Oncology ~$6.0B (+18%). Imfinzi ~$1.1B (+15%). Lynparza ~$500M (declining, expected). Datroway ~$20M in first partial quarters post-mBC launch (March 2025).

What AZ management said on the Q3 2025 call:

  1. "Our oncology franchise is the most diversified in the industry" — management framing AZ as a multi-asset oncology company, not a single-brand franchise. This is a direct counter to Keytruda's single-brand dominance narrative.
  2. Highlighted 8 ADCs in portfolio, 3 in Phase 3 — the ADC pipeline depth is positioned as AZ's next growth wave beyond IO. Datroway is the lead commercial asset but the messaging emphasizes the portfolio, not just one molecule.
  3. On Datroway: "Breast cancer is the beachhead; NSCLC data will define the franchise" — management explicitly signaling that the mBC launch is the starting point, not the destination. The 1L NSCLC TROPION trials are what determines whether Datroway becomes a blockbuster or a niche ADC.
  4. On POSEIDON commercial execution: "Message recall needs improvement; we're investing in rep training" — a rare public acknowledgment of promotional effectiveness gaps. This is directly corroborated by ZoomRx PET data showing POSEIDON message recall at 33% average.

Key analyst Q&A signals:

  1. Analysts pushed on Keytruda competitive pressure — AZ responded: "Imfinzi differentiates on long-term durability data (PACIFIC 5-year)." This confirms AZ's competitive strategy is niche data dominance, not head-to-head IO market share competition.
  2. Analysts pressed on Datroway launch trajectory — AZ deflected to future NSCLC data readouts. The mBC launch revenues ($20M) are modest but management is setting expectations for a slower breast cancer build with NSCLC as the inflection catalyst.
  3. Analysts asked about POSEIDON commercial execution — AZ's acknowledgment of "message recall needs improvement" is unusual candor that validates ZoomRx PET findings and suggests internal commercial analytics are aligned with external tracking data.

Competitor Earnings Intelligence

Revenue Snapshot — Q3 2025

Company Brand Q3 2025 Revenue YoY Growth Key Signal
Merck Keytruda ~$7.4B +15% Dominant IO; KN-689 perioperative HNSCC launched
BMS Opdivo + Opdualag ~$2.4B +5% #2 IO maintaining; CheckMate-77T perioperative NSCLC building
Gilead Trodelvy ~$350M -5% Declining in breast cancer; TROPION-Breast01 H2H data pressuring
GSK Zejula ~$80M -20% Declining post maintenance indication withdrawal
Imfinzi ~$1.5B +29% Context: Imfinzi is #3 IO globally behind Keytruda and Opdivo
Datroway Ramp Launch NSCLC accelerated approval June 2025
Lynparza ~$825M Flat Stable ~$3.3B run rate

Merck — Keytruda (~$7.4B, +15%)

Merck Q3 2025 earnings:

Keytruda's dominance across IO is the defining competitive reality for Imfinzi. Key themes:

  1. KN-689 launched in perioperative HNSCC: Merck continues to expand Keytruda into every IO indication with a perioperative play. The HNSCC launch signals Merck's strategy of making Keytruda "the backbone of oncology" — management used this exact phrase. Every perioperative expansion builds the muscle memory of prescribing Keytruda as the default IO choice, which spills over into NSCLC decisions.

  2. ~$7.4B quarterly makes Keytruda a ~$30B annual franchise: Keytruda generates roughly 5x Imfinzi's revenue in overlapping indications. The scale gap creates structural advantages in promotional reach, real-world evidence generation, and formulary positioning that are difficult to close through clinical data alone.

  3. Perioperative NSCLC is the direct collision point: KEYNOTE-671 (perioperative NSCLC) directly competes with AEGEAN/Imfinzi in the same setting. With Keytruda already dominant in metastatic NSCLC, the perioperative expansion leverages existing prescriber relationships — oncologists comfortable with Keytruda in mNSCLC naturally default to Keytruda in perioperative NSCLC.

Implication for AZ: Imfinzi's strongest competitive positions are where Keytruda doesn't have dominant data — Stage III unresectable (PACIFIC, long-term 5-year data) and LS-SCLC (ADRIATIC). POSEIDON's biomarker messaging (STK11m, KRASm) is the niche differentiation strategy against Keytruda's broad dominance. The Q3 data showing POSEIDON message traction is critical because it's the primary competitive counter.

BMS — Opdivo + Opdualag (~$2.4B, +5%)

BMS Q3 2025 earnings:

  1. Opdivo maintaining as #2 IO with 40+ approved indications: BMS emphasized the breadth of the Opdivo franchise. CheckMate-77T (perioperative NSCLC) is building momentum, adding another IO option in the setting where AEGEAN competes.

  2. Opdualag (nivolumab + relatlimab) growing in melanoma: ~$200M annual run-rate. While melanoma is not Imfinzi's core market, the IO combination approach validates the multi-agent IO strategy that AZ is also pursuing with Imjudo (tremelimumab).

  3. Perioperative IO competition intensifying: With Keytruda (KN-671), Opdivo (CM-77T), and Imfinzi (AEGEAN) all competing in perioperative NSCLC, the space is becoming crowded. Q3 PET data confirms all IO perioperative efficacy messages cluster in a narrow effectiveness range — differentiation is elusive.

Implication for AZ: Opdivo is less of a share threat than Keytruda but adds to the competitive noise in perioperative NSCLC. The AEGEAN effectiveness gap identified in Q3 PET data is partly due to this three-way competition making it harder for any single brand to break out.

Gilead — Trodelvy (~$350M, -5%)

Gilead Q3 2025 earnings:

  1. Trodelvy declining in breast cancer amid next-gen ADC competition: TROPION-Breast01 head-to-head data showing Dato-DXd superiority is directly driving Trodelvy's erosion. Gilead acknowledged competitive pressure from "next-gen ADCs" on the earnings call.

  2. TROP2 ADC class is expanding, not zero-sum: The total addressable market for TROP2-directed ADCs is growing as the class extends from TNBC into HR+/HER2- and eventually NSCLC. Trodelvy's decline is more about Datroway capturing growth than about market contraction.

  3. Trodelvy retains positions where Datroway data is less mature: Urothelial cancer and HR+/HER2- settings where Trodelvy has more mature data. Datroway's breast cancer and NSCLC focus creates a natural division — for now.

Implication for AZ: Trodelvy's Q3 decline validates AZ's ADC strategy with Datroway. The TROPION-Breast01 head-to-head data is the strongest competitive weapon AZ has in breast cancer, and Gilead's own acknowledgment of "next-gen ADC" pressure on earnings calls legitimizes Datroway's clinical narrative.

GSK — Zejula (~$80M, -20%)

  1. Zejula in managed decline: Following the maintenance indication withdrawal, Zejula is commercially winding down. GSK is deprioritizing the brand.

  2. PARP market consolidating around Lynparza: As Zejula exits, the PARP inhibitor market is increasingly a Lynparza (AZ/Merck) story, though Lynparza itself faces lifecycle challenges with next-gen combinations emerging.

Implication for AZ: Lynparza's declining revenue is a known trajectory, but Zejula's faster decline means Lynparza faces less PARP competition in the near term. The broader PARP class is transitioning from monotherapy maintenance to combination strategies — AZ's investment in next-gen combinations (e.g., PARP + IO, PARP + ADC) will determine whether the Lynparza revenue decline is managed or accelerated.

Synthesis: What the Combined Competitive Picture Tells Us (Q3 2025)

  1. Keytruda's ~$30B annual run rate vs Imfinzi's ~$6B is the structural reality — Imfinzi cannot compete head-to-head across all IO indications. The strategy must be niche dominance: PACIFIC (Stage III), ADRIATIC (LS-SCLC), POSEIDON biomarker subgroups — settings where Imfinzi has differentiated data.

  2. Perioperative NSCLC is a three-way fight with no clear winner on messaging — AEGEAN, KEYNOTE-671, and CheckMate-77T all cluster in a narrow effectiveness range. The PET data confirms that clinical differentiation is not translating to promotional differentiation.

  3. Datroway's competitive timing is excellent — launching into a TROP2 ADC market where the incumbent (Trodelvy) is declining. The NSCLC accelerated approval adds a growth vector that Trodelvy cannot match.

  4. Lynparza's revenue trajectory is manageable as Zejula declines faster — but the PARP franchise is a declining asset that needs strategic pivot to combinations.

What Matters for ZoomRx

  1. Imfinzi PET continues to be the flagship tracking engagement — Q3 data shows the POSEIDON biomarker messaging is gaining traction, validating the study design that tracks these subgroup messages
  2. Datroway NSCLC approval creates a new tracking opportunity — AZ started tracking Datroway in the NSCLC SOV basket from Jul 2025
  3. Pre-IMforte baseline in SCLC — Q3 2025 data becomes the critical benchmark for measuring IMforte's impact in subsequent quarters
  4. Dato-DXd 1L NSCLC qualitative research — ZoomRx's interim topline delivered; full global report in development with US, DE, FR, JP insights

Merck — Keytruda (~$7.4B, +15%)
Merck Q3 2025 earnings:
Keytruda's dominance across IO is the defining competitive reality for Imfinzi. Key themes:


KN-689 launched in perioperative HNSCC: Merck continues to expand Keytruda into every IO indication with a perioperative play. The HNSCC launch signals Merck's strategy of making Keytruda "the backbone of oncology" — management used this exact phrase. Every perioperative expansion builds the muscle memory of prescribing Keytruda as the default IO choice, which spills over into NSCLC decisions.


~$7.4B quarterly makes Keytruda a ~$30B annual franchise: Keytruda generates roughly 5x Imfinzi's revenue in overlapping indications. The scale gap creates structural advantages in promotional reach, real-world evidence generation, and formulary positioning that are difficult to close through clinical data alone.


Perioperative NSCLC is the direct collision point: KEYNOTE-671 (perioperative NSCLC) directly competes with AEGEAN/Imfinzi in the same setting. With Keytruda already dominant in metastatic NSCLC, the perioperative expansion leverages existing prescriber relationships — oncologists comfortable with Keytruda in mNSCLC naturally default to Keytruda in perioperative NSCLC.


Implication for AZ: Imfinzi's strongest competitive positions are where Keytruda doesn't have dominant data — Stage III unresectable (PACIFIC, long-term 5-year data) and LS-SCLC (ADRIATIC). POSEIDON's biomarker messaging (STK11m, KRASm) is the niche differentiation strategy against Keytruda's broad dominance. The Q3 data showing POSEIDON message traction is critical because it's the primary competitive counter.
BMS — Opdivo + Opdualag (~$2.4B, +5%)
BMS Q3 2025 earnings:


Opdivo maintaining as #2 IO with 40+ approved indications: BMS emphasized the breadth of the Opdivo franchise. CheckMate-77T (perioperative NSCLC) is building momentum, adding another IO option in the setting where AEGEAN competes.


Opdualag (nivolumab + relatlimab) growing in melanoma: ~$200M annual run-rate. While melanoma is not Imfinzi's core market, the IO combination approach validates the multi-agent IO strategy that AZ is also pursuing with Imjudo (tremelimumab).


Perioperative IO competition intensifying: With Keytruda (KN-671), Opdivo (CM-77T), and Imfinzi (AEGEAN) all competing in perioperative NSCLC, the space is becoming crowded. Q3 PET data confirms all IO perioperative efficacy messages cluster in a narrow effectiveness range — differentiation is elusive.


Implication for AZ: Opdivo is less of a share threat than Keytruda but adds to the competitive noise in perioperative NSCLC. The AEGEAN effectiveness gap identified in Q3 PET data is partly due to this three-way competition making it harder for any single brand to break out.
Gilead — Trodelvy (~$350M, -5%)
Gilead Q3 2025 earnings:


Trodelvy declining in breast cancer amid next-gen ADC competition: TROPION-Breast01 head-to-head data showing Dato-DXd superiority is directly driving Trodelvy's erosion. Gilead acknowledged competitive pressure from "next-gen ADCs" on the earnings call.


TROP2 ADC class is expanding, not zero-sum: The total addressable market for TROP2-directed ADCs is growing as the class extends from TNBC into HR+/HER2- and eventually NSCLC. Trodelvy's decline is more about Datroway capturing growth than about market contraction.


Trodelvy retains positions where Datroway data is less mature: Urothelial cancer and HR+/HER2- settings where Trodelvy has more mature data. Datroway's breast cancer and NSCLC focus creates a natural division — for now.


Implication for AZ: Trodelvy's Q3 decline validates AZ's ADC strategy with Datroway. The TROPION-Breast01 head-to-head data is the strongest competitive weapon AZ has in breast cancer, and Gilead's own acknowledgment of "next-gen ADC" pressure on earnings calls legitimizes Datroway's clinical narrative.
GSK — Zejula (~$80M, -20%)


Zejula in managed decline: Following the maintenance indication withdrawal, Zejula is commercially winding down. GSK is deprioritizing the brand.


PARP market consolidating around Lynparza: As Zejula exits, the PARP inhibitor market is increasingly a Lynparza (AZ/Merck) story, though Lynparza itself faces lifecycle challenges with next-gen combinations emerging.


Implication for AZ: Lynparza's declining revenue is a known trajectory, but Zejula's faster decline means Lynparza faces less PARP competition in the near term. The broader PARP class is transitioning from monotherapy maintenance to combination strategies — AZ's investment in next-gen combinations (e.g., PARP + IO, PARP + ADC) will determine whether the Lynparza revenue decline is managed or accelerated.
Synthesis: What the Combined Competitive Picture Tells Us (Q3 2025)


Keytruda's ~$30B annual run rate vs Imfinzi's ~$6B is the structural reality — Imfinzi cannot compete head-to-head across all IO indications. The strategy must be niche dominance: PACIFIC (Stage III), ADRIATIC (LS-SCLC), POSEIDON biomarker subgroups — settings where Imfinzi has differentiated data.


Perioperative NSCLC is a three-way fight with no clear winner on messaging — AEGEAN, KEYNOTE-671, and CheckMate-77T all cluster in a narrow effectiveness range. The PET data confirms that clinical differentiation is not translating to promotional differentiation.


Datroway's competitive timing is excellent — launching into a TROP2 ADC market where the incumbent (Trodelvy) is declining. The NSCLC accelerated approval adds a growth vector that Trodelvy cannot match.


Lynparza's revenue trajectory is manageable as Zejula declines faster — but the PARP franchise is a declining asset that needs strategic pivot to combinations.
